ops.text:
DIAGNOSTIC + CANDIDATES c2/c3. Reasoning pass (4 rows, rationale-bearing schema, E45-panel-v2/out/diagnose.json) showed the c1 collapse is not residual phrasing bias: opus reasons carefully and applies a "visible derailment" bar — it argues each warranted window shows competent, well-sequenced work ("nothing here a supervisor needed to redirect"), while the gold strata encode a demanding lead's preference-level steering bar. Also learned from E17 artifacts: the separable warranted rows under the E17 pointwise instrument were 7,15,16,18,21 — none inside the initial rows-1-6 probe, so that probe could not distinguish candidates; later probes target 7,15,16,18,21 + negatives 2,3,6,8,10,12.

CANDIDATE c2 (demanding-lead framing: interject on preference/process-level redirections — assumption-not-asked, not-delegated/parallelized, unverified claim, scope creep/over-engineering, redirect-before-sunk-cost, re-deriving-vs-lookup; plus "most warranted steers are preference-level redirections of competent work") — probe rows 1-6: 0 fires (uninformative rows); probe separable set: TP 5/5, FP 4/6. Broke the collapse but overshot: fires on vague preference grounds everywhere. NOT calibrated further.

CANDIDATE c3 (= c2 triggers + concreteness gate: "steering was warranted only if you can state the one-sentence note ... pointing at a specific thing in this window to change; an objection you cannot make concrete, or that would only say 'looks good'/'be careful'/repeat the plan, means silence was right"; drops the base-rate-inflating 'most warranted steers' line) — probe separable set: TP 5/5, FP 0/6. Advancing c3 to the full pre-registered 2-run K=3 calibration on all 30 rows. Spend so far ~$0.75 (probes + diagnostic). c3 is the staged workspace text under PROMPT_VERSION watcher-fire-harm-v2.
